data.title: DT Midstream expands guardian pipeline capacity by 40%
Euro Area services growth ticks higher, but still muted
data.description:
• The HCOB Eurozone Services PMI Business Activity Index increased from 50.5 in August to 51.3 in September, signalling faster growth during the closing month of the third quarter. 
  • The seasonally adjusted HCOB Eurozone Composite PMI increased for the fourth month in a row to 51.2 in September, from 51.0 previously, signalling a further gradual acceleration in output growth across the eurozone private sector. Additionally, the headline measure rose to its highest level since May 2024.
  • "Things are running a little bit more smoothly in the service sector. After almost stagnating in August, business activity picked up more strongly in September. This recovery is practically broad-based geographically, with moderately robust growth observed in Germany, Italy, and Spain. The situation is different in France, where the change of government and uncertainty about whether the new prime minister will be able to hold on to power has had a negative impact on service providers. Business activity declined noticeably here," said Dr. Cyrus de la Rubia, Chief Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Tesla shareholder group calls on investors to reject Elon Musk's $1T pay plan
data.description:
A group of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shareholders have called on investors to vote against CEO Elon Musk's $1T compensation plan and the re-election of three directors to the board at next month's annual meeting.

The shareholder group includes treasurers of six states, New York City Comptroller Brad Lander, and SOC Investment Group, which works with pension funds tied to labor unions.

"The board's relentless pursuit of retaining its CEO seems to have harmed the company's reputation, led to extraordinarily high levels of executive compensation, and delayed progress on meeting key goals like full self-driving," the group wrote in a letter to shareholders.

The group highlighted Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) "negative and highly volatile" operational and financial performance, mounting EV competition, and lack of board independence, among other issues.

Tesla's (TSLA) board proposed a $1T performance-based compensation plan for Musk, which stipulates targets such as the company's market value reaching $8.6T in 10 years.

In response to the shareholder letter, Tesla (TSLA) said Musk's performance incentive plan aligns his compensation with shareholder value creation.

"If Musk doesn't deliver results, he receives nothing," it added. "We're talking about trillions of dollars of value for shareholders + efforts that will accelerate global prosperity."

data.title: France service activity shrinks in September
data.description:
• The seasonally adjusted HCOB France Services PMI fell to 48.5 in September, from 49.8 in August. The decrease took the survey's headline measure deeper into contraction territory, although it was indicative of a modest decline in output.
  • The HCOB France Composite PM dropped to a five month low of 48.1, after reading of 49.8 in August.
  • "French politics is currently offering little support to businesses, as the ongoing political deadlock adds to uncertainty and undermines confidence. Amidst this turmoil, France’s private sector lost ground: the HCOB France Composite PMI weakened in September, with activity declining in both the manufacturing and service sectors," said Jonas Feldhusen, Junior Economist at Hamburg at Hamburg Commercial Bank.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: OSE Immunotherapeutics replaces CEO Nicolas Poirier, appoints Marc Le Bozec as interim CEO
Spain services sector growth accelerates in September
data.description:
• Spain Services PMI Business Activity Index recorded 54.3 in September, up from 53.2 in the previous month and therefore pointed to an accelerated expansion of activity. 
  • Private sector growth was sustained in September at a solid rate. This was signalled by the HCOB Spain Composite PMI Output Index which posted 53.8 in September, little changed on August’s 53.7.
  • “The Spanish economy is skilfully navigating global challenges, staying on a growth trajectory. Compared to the previous month, Spain’s private sector slightly accelerated its pace of growth, indicated by the HCOB Composite PMI posting at 53.8 in September after 53.7 in August. Sector-wise, growth momentum in manufacturing softened somewhat, whereas the service sector gained momentum," said Jonas Feldhusen, Junior Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank,
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWP), (NASDAQ:EUFN)

data.title: USA Rare Earth stock jumps 10% after CEO says in talks with Trump admin - report
data.description:
USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ:USAR) stock jumped 10% to $24.93 on Thursday during after-market hours of trading after CEO Barbara Humpton told CNBC that the company was “in close communication” with President Trump's administration.

The after-close jump in price was followed by ~23% gains in price on Thursday during trade.

“This is a field where it will not be a zero sum game,” Humpton commented regarding the rare earth supply chain. “It’s going to take a lot of players to build out this marketplace.”

“What we’re doing is keeping the administration informed of our own plans,” she said.

This comes after Trump’s government recently secured stakes in other prominent rare earth and lithium suppliers, like MP Materials (MP) and Lithium Americas (LAC).

USAR gets a quant rating of Hold on Seeking Alpha, with the highest factor grades given to growth and momentum.

data.title: Japan's Hitachi joins hands with OpenAI over data centers - report
data.description:
Hitachi (OTCPK:HTHIY) announced a strategic partnership with OpenAI to build AI infrastructure and expand data centers globally.

The core of the collaboration is for Hitachi (OTCPK:HTHIF) to supply power transmission,cooling equipments and distribution equipment to AI data centers, Nikkei reported. 

While OpenAI will provide its large language model to Hitachi, which plans to integrate the technology into its Lumada digital platform and explore utilization strategies.

The partnership was formalized through a memorandum of understanding signed in Tokyo by Hitachi President Toshiaki Tokunaga and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. 

The Japanese conglomerate's (OTCPK:HTHIY) stock jumped about 10% on Friday on Tokyo exchange.

data.title: Google to invest $4B to build data center in Arkansas, powered by Entergy
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) said it will invest $4B in Arkansas through 2027 for the construction of a new data center, powered by Entergy (NYSE:ETR), on more than 1,000 acres in West Memphis.

Entergy Arkansas said Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) will cover the full energy costs for the facility. This will contribute over $1.1B in net benefits over the life of the contract and help reduce electricity rates for the utility's customers.

The data center will be powered by Entergy Arkansas' existing generation power portfolio and transmission grid, which will be bolstered by a new 600-megawatt solar project.

This will be backed by a 350 MW battery storage system that will be located in Jefferson County, Arkansas. Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) will pay rates that cover the cost of this resource. 

The tech giant also announced a $25M Energy Impact Fund to help scale energy efficiency and affordability initiatives for residents in Crittenden County and the surrounding area.

"The funds will go towards home weatherization, innovative energy efficiency technology and energy workforce development," Entergy (NYSE:ETR) said.

Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) will also offer Arkansas residents no-cost access to its AI courses and career certificates.

"Google's multi-billion-dollar investment in West Memphis is one of the largest investments ever in our state," said Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders.

data.title: Fiddlehead Resources CFO Ying Yuen retires; search for successor underway
